forbiddenPatterns: trap wordings such as 'Akceptuję i przechodzę...'

enforcement.style: grace period expired 30/09/2025 → sanction phase OPEN; triple regulator, dark patterns up to 10% of turnover (consumer-protection track)

productImplications: Commercial timing: a market just discovering sanctions — 'zgodność PKE' GEO content with strong potential
lawyerFlags: triple regulator UODO/UKE/UOKiK: division of competence to be clarified (UKE can fine up to 3% of turnover)

validated.note: UODO: e-commerce guide (Poradnik, 03/2025) — explicitly PROHIBITS 'Accept and go to the shop' without an equivalent 'Reject all'; ONE-click refusal as easy as accepting (parity, ref. CJEU C-21/23); banner specs (≤ 20% of screen, 5 s collapse, bottom bar on mobile); active opt-in (browser settings/scrolling invalid). Flag 'first-layer refusal not codified' RESOLVED → promoted to high.

banner.notes: on a blocking banner, an easy-to-use close CROSS is MANDATORY = closing without making a choice, zero cookies set

enforcement.style: final record cookie fine of 898 000 CZK

productImplications: Close-cross state machine: 3rd behavior (mandatory easy close, nothing recorded)
lawyerFlags: 12/6-month durations = market practice, not set by the ÚOOÚ (FAQ guidance, with no explicit maximum duration)

validated.note: §89(3) of Act (zákona) 127/2005 Sb. (opt-in since 01/01/2022, aligned with Art. 5(3) ePrivacy): prior consent mandatory except for strictly necessary cookies. ÚOOÚ = single authority, monitoring report + FAQ, active audits (record fine 898 000 CZK). Opt-in confirmed.

sk.legalBasis: ePrivacy transposition (Electronic Communications Act) + GDPR; first ÚOOÚ cookie guidelines (updated)

banner.notes: Aligned with the GDPR standard after ÚOOÚ intervention; refusal as easy as acceptance (EDPB baseline)

enforcement.style: The ÚOOÚ has so far AVOIDED the cookies topic in its supervision — decisions and fines absent (low immediate risk but liable to evolve)

productImplications: EDPB-strict preset; weak enforcement but align with the converging doctrine
lawyerFlags: ÚOOÚ guidance 09/11/2022: opt-in (switch 02/2022), color parity required, consent per purpose. Final lawyer review required.

validated.note: Official authority guidance verified. Final lawyer review required before any 'compliant' claim.

hu.legalBasis: Act C of 2003 on electronic communications + GDPR; NAIH decision-making practice

banner.notes: 'Reject all' and 'Accept all' with the SAME prominence, at the same level (making refusal harder = dark pattern according to NAIH)

analyticsExemption.notes: marketing and analytics cookies = consent; legitimate interest reserved for the controller's essential cookies

enforcement.style: first cookie sanction in 2022 (TV2 Media, ~€25k); NAIH criticizes the misleading use of legitimate interest

lawyerFlags: maximum validity duration / close-cross behavior not quantified by the NAIH

validated.note: NAIH: 'Accept all'/'Reject all' parity at the SAME level explicitly REQUIRED (making refusal harder = unlawful); separate consent per purpose; withdrawal as easy as granting. Real enforcement (TV2 fine, NAIH-3195-11/2022, 10 M HUF). Promoted to high.

ro.legalBasis: Law 506/2004 (+ DRAFT amendment of 27/04/2026, not adopted)

enforcement.style: series of 15-30 k RON fines in late 2025-2026 (cookies set without consent); up to 2% of turnover if turnover > 5 M RON

productImplications: Encode the DRAFT's standard now + entry-into-force tracker (120-day compliance period planned)
lawyerFlags: first-layer refusal parity already follows from ANSPDCP + EDPB guidance (enforced via fines); the 27/04/2026 draft amendment would codify it — track its adoption

validated.note: Law 506/2004 (ePrivacy transposition): express consent required; ANSPDCP guidance = full refusal option in the first layer as visible/accessible as acceptance, one-click refusal, 'you can customize' does not replace a reject button. Confirmed. Sanctions up to 4% of turnover.
